wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the SurveillanceQT project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a real-time environmental monitoring and security surveillance system for distributed Raspberry Pi sensor networks. Document the key features including multi-level user authentication, real-time sensor monitoring, camera surveillance, network discovery, and drag-and-drop widget system. Describe the target audience, system requirements, and main architectural components. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical highlights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the Qt framework and embedded systems domain.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the design patterns implemented in SurveillanceQT. Detail the Factory pattern implementation in SensorFactory for creating specialized sensor widgets (temperature, smoke, etc.). Explain the Singleton pattern usage in DatabaseManager for centralized database access and connection management. Document the Observer pattern implementation using Qt's signal-slot mechanism for real-time data updates and event handling. Describe the MVC pattern structure with DashboardWindow as controller, individual sensor widgets as views, and DatabaseManager/RaspberryManager as models. Include pattern benefits, implementation details, and how these patterns enhance system modularity and maintainability.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication system implementation. Document the LoginWidget component including form validation, input sanitization, and user interface behavior. Explain the authentication workflow from login attempt to successful authentication or failure handling. Detail the password verification process using hashed passwords stored in the database. Cover the AuthenticationDialog functionality for password changes and user registration. Document the signal-slot connections between authentication components and error handling mechanisms. Include security measures such as input validation, SQL injection prevention, and secure password comparison techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sensor Factory Pattern Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SensorFactory pattern implementation. Explain the factory method pattern used to create specialized sensor widgets including SmokeSensorWidget, TemperatureWidget, and CameraWidget. Document the SensorType enumeration and its mapping to different sensor categories. Detail the SensorConfig structure and its role in sensor configuration management. Explain factory methods for creating sensors with default configurations, including default names, units, and threshold values. Document the static utility methods for sensor type conversion, icon assignment, and configuration defaults. Include practical examples of sensor creation workflows and integration patterns with the dashboard system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Drag-and-Drop Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the drag-and-drop interface implementation. Explain the WidgetEditor class architecture and its role in managing widget placement and configuration. Document the drag operation mechanics, mouse event handling, and widget positioning algorithms. Detail the WidgetConfig structure and how it stores widget properties including ID, name, type, thresholds, unit, and enabled status. Explain the dialog-based configuration system for customizing widget properties including name editing, type selection, threshold setting, and unit specification. Include practical examples of widget creation, movement, and configuration workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: ARP Scanner Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ARP scanner implementation. Explain the ArpScanner class architecture, including the NetworkDevice and KnownRaspberryPi data structures. Document the ARP scanning algorithms, ping sweep functionality, and host discovery mechanisms. Detail the MAC address vendor identification system, device type classification algorithms, and hostname resolution processes. Include the scanning strategies for local subnets, timeout handling, and progress tracking. Cover the integration with Qt's signal-slot mechanism for real-time scan updates and error handling. Provide examples of scan initiation, device parsing, and result processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: MQTT Broker Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for MQTT broker management within the Raspberry Pi network system. Detail the BrokerConfig structure including host, port, protocol, username, and password settings. Explain the MQTT connection establishment process, authentication mechanisms, and secure communication protocols. Document connection management including auto-connect on startup, reconnection intervals, and heartbeat mechanisms. Cover quality of service considerations, topic management for sensor data publishing and subscribing, and connection reliability features. Include configuration examples, troubleshooting connection issues, and best practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Components API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the core system components in SurveillanceQT. Document the DashboardWindow class as the central controller, including all public methods, signals, slots, and properties. Detail the DatabaseManager singleton pattern implementation with database connection methods, query execution functions, and user management APIs. Cover the RaspberryManager class for MQTT broker coordination, node management, and sensor network communication. Include the ModuleManager for system module loading and management. Provide complete method signatures, parameter descriptions, return values, and error handling. Include practical usage examples showing how these components interact and integrate with the overall system architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for SurveillanceQT. Include detailed prerequisites covering Qt 5.15+, MySQL/MariaDB, and Raspberry Pi setup requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for Windows, Linux, and Raspberry Pi platforms. Document the build process using qmake and the applicationmalette.pro configuration. Explain first run configuration including database initialization, MQTT broker setup, and sensor network configuration. Include basic usage tutorial covering login procedures, dashboard navigation, and initial sensor setup. Provide troubleshooting tips for common installation issues and verification steps to ensure proper system setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for component interactions within the SurveillanceQT architecture. Explain the communication flow between DashboardWindow as the central coordinator and its managed components including DatabaseManager, RaspberryManager, SensorFactory, and ArpScanner. Document data flow patterns from sensor data collection through MQTT to widget updates, user authentication through database verification, and network discovery results to UI updates. Detail the event-driven architecture using Qt signals and slots for inter-component communication. Include sequence diagrams showing typical workflows like user login, sensor data processing, and network scanning operations. Address error propagation, exception handling, and component lifecycle management.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the role-based access control system. Detail the three user roles: Admin (full rights), Operator (medium rights for modifying certain parameters), and Viewer (read-only access). Document the UserRole enum implementation and permission checking methods including hasPermission(), canEditWidgets(), canManageModules(), canConfigureSystem(), and canViewSensors(). Explain the User struct data model and role-to-string conversion utilities. Cover role assignment during user creation and runtime role validation throughout the application. Include examples of permission checks and role-based UI element visibility controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supported Sensor Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for all supported sensor types in the surveillance system. Document SmokeSensorWidget for environmental smoke detection, TemperatureWidget for temperature monitoring, and CameraWidget for video surveillance. Explain the specific functionality, data processing, and visualization methods for each sensor type. Detail sensor data collection mechanisms, real-time updates, and display formatting. Document sensor-specific configuration options, threshold management, and alert triggering. Include implementation details for sensor widget rendering, user interaction patterns, and integration with the factory system. Provide examples of sensor instantiation and configuration for each type.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resize System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the resize system implementation. Explain the ResizableHelper class functionality for enabling dynamic widget resizing, including size constraint enforcement and aspect ratio maintenance. Document the ResizeHandle component implementation for user-driven resizing operations, including mouse interaction handling and visual feedback mechanisms. Detail the resize algorithm implementation, boundary detection, and smooth resizing animations. Include the integration with the Qt layout system and how resize operations affect widget positioning within the dashboard grid. Provide examples of resize configuration and customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Device Classification and Recognition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for device classification and recognition systems. Document the device identification algorithms including MAC address prefix matching, hostname analysis, and vendor identification. Explain the KnownRaspberryPi detection system, surveillance module classification, and custom device type recognition. Detail the classification criteria for different device categories, the threshold-based confidence scoring, and the integration with known device databases. Include examples of device categorization workflows, custom classification rules, and the handling of unknown or ambiguous devices. Address performance considerations for large-scale device classification and accuracy optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Node Configuration and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Raspberry Pi node configuration and management. Explain the RaspberryNode structure including id, name, ipAddress, macAddress, description, sensors array, and extraConfig fields. Document the SensorInfo structure with sensor types (Temperature_DHT22, Humidity_DHT22, Smoke_MQ2, AirQuality_PIM480, VOC_PIM480, Camera), topics, units, thresholds, and extra configurations. Cover node lifecycle management including adding, updating, removing nodes, and status tracking. Detail the JSON configuration format, sensor type enumeration, and node discovery mechanisms. Include practical examples of node configuration files, sensor assignment patterns, and troubleshooting configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Classes API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for utility classes that support the main system functionality. Document the ArpScanner class for network device discovery, including scanning methods, device classification algorithms, and ARP table analysis functions. Cover the SensorFactory class implementing factory pattern for creating specialized sensor widgets, including factory methods and widget creation APIs. Detail the ResizableHelper and ResizeHandle classes for drag-and-drop interface functionality, including resize operations, event handling, and positioning calculations. Provide method signatures, parameter specifications, return values, and integration examples. Include usage patterns for extending functionality and customizing behavior.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the SurveillanceQT system. Describe the high-level design showing the MVC pattern implementation with Qt framework. Document the main architectural components including DashboardWindow as the central controller, DatabaseManager singleton for data persistence, RaspberryManager for MQTT coordination, SensorFactory for widget creation, and ArpScanner for network discovery. Explain component interactions, data flows between modules, and integration patterns. Detail the design patterns used including Factory pattern for sensors, Observer pattern via Qt signals, and Singleton pattern for database access. Include system boundaries, external dependencies, and architectural decisions with trade-offs and constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data flow architecture in SurveillanceQT. Detail the complete data pipeline from sensor data acquisition through Raspberry Pi nodes to real-time display in widgets. Document the MQTT message flow from sensor nodes to RaspberryManager and then to DashboardWindow for UI updates. Explain database data flow for user management, audit logging, and configuration storage. Include data transformation processes, caching strategies, and real-time update mechanisms. Document error handling in data transmission, data validation at each stage, and fallback mechanisms. Provide diagrams illustrating typical data flow scenarios including sensor readings, user authentication, and network status updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Password Security and Hashing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for password security implementation. Document the password hashing mechanism using Qt's cryptographic functions and explain why standard hash functions are not used. Detail the hashPassword() method implementation and security considerations for password storage. Explain the password change process including old password verification and new password validation. Cover password strength requirements and validation rules. Document the security benefits of using Qt's built-in cryptographic functions versus manual hashing implementations. Include best practices for password security and recommendations for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sensor Configuration and Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for sensor configuration and management systems. Explain the SensorConfig structure and its fields including sensor ID, name, type, warning thresholds, alarm thresholds, and units. Document the AddSensorDialog interface for configuring new sensors including parameter input, validation, and creation workflow. Detail threshold management systems for warning and alarm conditions. Explain sensor naming conventions, unit systems, and configuration persistence. Document the relationship between sensor configuration and widget creation through the factory pattern. Include examples of sensor configuration workflows, validation rules, and error handling during sensor setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Edit Mode and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the edit mode and configuration system. Explain how widgets enter and exit edit mode, including the visual indicators and user interaction patterns. Document the WidgetEditor dialog implementation for property modification, including form controls for name editing, type selection, threshold configuration, and unit specification. Detail the WidgetConfig structure and how it facilitates serialization/deserialization of widget properties. Explain the camera mode functionality and special configuration handling for camera widgets. Include validation rules, default value handling, and error prevention mechanisms in the configuration process.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Scanner Dialog Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the NetworkScannerDialog user interface implementation. Detail the dialog layout, user interaction patterns, and control functionality for network scanning operations. Document the scan initiation controls, progress indicators, results display mechanisms, and device filtering capabilities. Explain the integration with the ArpScanner backend, real-time result updates, and user feedback systems. Include the dialog's role in the overall surveillance system, user workflow considerations, and accessibility features. Cover the dialog's state management, error handling, and user guidance mechanisms. Provide examples of dialog usage, customization options, and integration with the main application window.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Discovery Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for integrating network discovery with Raspberry Pi node management. Explain how ArpScanner works with RaspberryManager to automatically discover and register network devices. Document the device classification process, MAC address vendor identification, and known device recognition. Cover the real-time network topology mapping, automatic node registration based on discovered devices, and status synchronization between network scans and node management. Detail the integration points between ArpScanner and RaspberryManager, including data exchange formats and update mechanisms. Include configuration options for discovery intervals, filtering criteria, and troubleshooting discovery failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all user interface components in SurveillanceQT. Document the SmokeSensorWidget and TemperatureWidget classes for displaying environmental sensor data, including update methods, threshold visualization, and real-time data binding. Cover the CameraWidget for video streaming functionality, including camera initialization, stream management, and display controls. Detail the LoginWidget and AuthenticationDialog classes for user authentication, including login validation, role-based access control, and session management APIs. Document the NetworkScannerDialog and AddSensorDialog for network management interfaces, including device discovery, configuration dialogs, and user input handling. Include the WidgetEditor class for drag-and-drop configuration, covering edit mode functionality and widget manipulation APIs. Provide complete method signatures, signal-slot connections, property bindings, and usage examples for each component.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user management system. Detail the multi-level authentication system with Admin, Operator, and Viewer roles. Document the password hashing implementation using Qt's cryptographic functions and security compliance measures. Explain the user registration process, role assignment, and permission hierarchies. Cover the audit logging system for tracking user activities and security compliance requirements. Document the LoginWidget implementation including form validation, session management, and security measures. Include user administration interface functionality and best practices for user account management.

wiki_catalogs.name: External Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the external dependencies and system requirements for SurveillanceQT. Detail the Qt framework dependencies including Qt Core, Qt GUI, Qt SQL, and Qt Network modules. Explain MySQL/MariaDB database integration requirements and configuration. Document MQTT library dependencies for real-time sensor communication. Include build system requirements, compiler versions, and platform-specific considerations. Detail runtime dependencies, shared libraries, and system prerequisites. Explain dependency management through Qt's qmake/pro file system. Address version compatibility requirements, upgrade paths, and troubleshooting common dependency issues. Include installation procedures for all required components and verification steps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Logging and Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the audit logging system. Detail the logAction() method implementation for tracking user activities and system events. Document the audit trail structure including username, action type, timestamp, and optional details. Explain the security compliance aspects of maintaining activity logs and regulatory requirements. Cover log retention policies and database storage considerations. Document the integration with user authentication events and system operations. Include examples of logged activities such as login attempts, user management actions, and configuration changes. Address log viewing capabilities and security implications of audit trails.

wiki_catalogs.name: Alerting and Threshold Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the alerting and threshold management system. Explain the threshold-based alerting mechanism including warning thresholds and alarm thresholds for different sensor types. Document how threshold violations trigger alerts and notifications throughout the system. Detail the relationship between sensor readings and alert states. Explain threshold configuration options, validation rules, and dynamic threshold updates. Document alert visualization methods, status indicators, and user notification systems. Include examples of threshold configuration for different sensor types and alert response workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Overlay and Lock System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the security overlay and lock system implementation for protecting widget configurations. Explain the lock overlay mechanism that prevents unauthorized modifications to critical widgets, including visual indicators and user permission checks. Detail the integration with the authentication system and how different user roles affect widget access and modification capabilities. Document the security validation processes, permission checking algorithms, and enforcement mechanisms. Include examples of secure widget management workflows and best practices for maintaining system integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Network Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for real-time network monitoring capabilities. Document the continuous monitoring systems, automatic scan scheduling, and dynamic network topology tracking. Explain the surveillance module detection, online/offline status tracking, and RSSI monitoring for wireless devices. Detail the integration with the dashboard system, alert generation for network changes, and historical network activity logging. Include the monitoring performance optimization, resource management, and scalability considerations for large networks. Cover the monitoring data structures, update mechanisms, and integration with the broader surveillance monitoring system. Provide examples of monitoring configurations, alert thresholds, and troubleshooting network monitoring issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Monitoring and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for network monitoring and maintenance operations in the Raspberry Pi sensor network. Detail the node status tracking mechanisms including online/offline detection, last seen timestamps, and heartbeat monitoring. Explain the nodeStatusChanged signal and its integration with the dashboard interface for real-time status updates. Document fault tolerance strategies, automatic recovery mechanisms, and connection resilience features. Cover performance monitoring, network latency tracking, and bandwidth optimization techniques. Include maintenance procedures for node cleanup, orphaned device removal, and network health assessment. Provide troubleshooting guides for common network issues, connectivity problems, and performance degradation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the SurveillanceQT database schema. Detail all database tables including users, sensor_data, raspberry_nodes, audit_logs, and configuration tables. Document entity relationships, primary/foreign keys, indexes, and constraints. Explain the relational structure and data integrity rules. Include database initialization process, default data population, and schema evolution strategies. Document the DatabaseManager implementation including connection pooling, query execution patterns, and error handling. Provide examples of common queries and data access patterns used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the user administration functionality. Document the user management methods including createUser(), getUser(), getAllUsers(), deactivateUser(), and updateLastLogin(). Explain the default user creation process and initial system setup. Detail the user data model with fields for username, password, role, full name, email, active status, and timestamps. Cover the session management system including setCurrentUser(), clearCurrentUser(), and isUserLoggedIn() functionality. Document the user listing and filtering capabilities. Include examples of common administrative tasks such as creating new users, updating user roles, and deactivating accounts. Address user data validation and error handling throughout the administration workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sensor Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the sensor management system. Explain the factory pattern implementation in SensorFactory for creating specialized sensor widgets including SmokeSensorWidget, TemperatureWidget, and CameraWidget. Document sensor configuration management, threshold-based alerting system, and real-time data processing. Detail the different sensor types supported including environmental sensors (temperature, humidity, smoke, CO2, VOC) and camera surveillance. Explain sensor data collection, processing, and visualization. Include sensor configuration dialogs, alert thresholds, and notification systems. Document the integration with Raspberry Pi sensor networks and MQTT communication protocols.

wiki_catalogs.name: Raspberry Pi Network Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Raspberry Pi network management. Detail the MQTT broker configuration and connection management for coordinating distributed sensor networks. Document the node discovery and registration process, connection management and recovery mechanisms, and network topology management. Explain the RaspberryPi node configuration using JSON format including broker settings, sensor assignments, and network parameters. Cover the integration with ArpScanner for automatic device discovery and status tracking. Include network monitoring, fault tolerance, and performance optimization strategies for distributed sensor deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Widget System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the widget system architecture. Explain the drag-and-drop interface design implementation, widget factory pattern for dynamic component creation, and resize handle functionality. Document the edit mode and configuration system, lock overlay security features, and widget positioning algorithms. Detail the ResizableHelper implementation for dynamic sizing and the ResizeHandle component for user interaction. Include the WidgetEditor's role in managing widget properties, configuration dialogs, and user customization options. Explain the integration with the dashboard layout system and event handling mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the dashboard interface components. Describe the main window layout and components including the title bar, bottom status bar, and sensor container management. Document the DashboardWindow implementation as the central controller, event handling and user interactions, and integration with all system components. Explain the MainWindow UI design, window controls, and user experience considerations. Include the layout management system, responsive design principles, and accessibility features. Document the integration points with authentication, sensor management, and network discovery systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Network Discovery System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the network discovery system. Detail the ARP scanner implementation for device classification and recognition, network scanning strategies, and real-time network monitoring. Document the NetworkScannerDialog functionality including scan initiation, results display, and device classification algorithms. Explain MAC address vendor identification, known device recognition, and network topology mapping. Include the integration with Raspberry Pi detection, network security scanning, and device status tracking. Cover performance optimization for large networks and troubleshooting network discovery issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for configuration management systems. Explain the JSON configuration structure for Raspberry Pi nodes including broker settings, sensor assignments, and network parameters. Document the configuration validation and error handling mechanisms, default configuration templates, and configuration migration strategies. Detail the AddSensorDialog implementation for dynamic sensor configuration, configuration persistence, and runtime configuration updates. Include best practices for configuration management, security considerations for sensitive settings, and troubleshooting configuration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Considerations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security documentation for SurveillanceQT. Detail the password hashing and storage implementation using Qt's cryptographic functions, role-based access control system, and audit trail implementation for compliance requirements. Document secure communication protocols for MQTT connections, session management and timeout handling, and security best practices for production deployment. Explain the security measures in authentication dialogs, input validation and sanitization, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Include security audit guidelines, penetration testing recommendations, and incident response procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all public interfaces in SurveillanceQT. Document all classes including DashboardWindow, DatabaseManager, SensorFactory, RaspberryManager, ArpScanner, WidgetEditor, and their respective methods, parameters, and return values. Include signal and slot definitions, public member functions, and property declarations. Detail the class hierarchies, inheritance relationships, and interface contracts. Provide method signatures with parameter descriptions, return value specifications, and exception handling. Include usage examples for each major API component, integration patterns, and best practices for extending the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development and Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for SurveillanceQT. Detail the development environment setup including Qt Creator configuration, MySQL development database setup, and cross-platform compilation. Document the build configuration using qmake, dependency management, and project structure organization. Explain testing strategies including unit testing, integration testing, and end-to-end testing approaches. Cover debugging techniques using Qt Creator, database debugging, and network communication debugging. Include code quality standards, coding conventions, and best practices for Qt/C++ development. Document the development workflow, version control practices, and contribution guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and maintenance documentation for SurveillanceQT. Detail production deployment procedures for Windows, Linux, and Raspberry Pi platforms including system requirements, dependency installation, and service configuration. Document system monitoring and maintenance procedures, backup and recovery strategies for database and configuration files, and performance tuning guidelines. Explain update procedures, version management, and rollback strategies. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues, log analysis techniques, and system health monitoring. Cover security hardening procedures, performance optimization, and capacity planning for large-scale deployments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# SurveillanceQT: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
SurveillanceQT is a sophisticated Qt-based surveillance monitoring system designed for real-time environmental monitoring and security surveillance. The project provides a comprehensive dashboard interface for managing multiple sensor types including temperature, humidity, smoke detection, air quality monitoring, and camera surveillance across distributed Raspberry Pi nodes.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Continuous monitoring of environmental conditions and security parameters
- **Distributed Architecture**: Support for multiple Raspberry Pi nodes with MQTT communication
- **Multi-level Security**: Role-based access control with three distinct user roles (Admin, Operator, Viewer)
- **Flexible Widget System**: Dynamic sensor widgets with drag-and-drop functionality
- **Network Discovery**: Automated network scanning and device identification capabilities

### Target Audience
- Security personnel and operators requiring real-time monitoring capabilities
- IT administrators managing distributed sensor networks
- Facilities management teams overseeing environmental conditions
- Educational institutions studying IoT and embedded systems integration

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Application] --> B[Dashboard Window]
    B --> C[Login Widget]
    B --> D[Sensor Widgets]
    B --> E[Network Scanner]
    B --> F[Module Manager]
    
    D --> G[Smoke Sensor Widget]
    D --> H[Temperature Widget]
    D --> I[Camera Widget]
    
    B --> J[Database Manager]
    B --> K[Raspberry Manager]
    B --> L[ARP Scanner]
    
    K --> M[MQTT Broker]
    K --> N[Raspberry Pi Nodes]
    
    J --> O[(MySQL Database)]
```

### Design Patterns

The project implements several key design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern**: Used in `SensorFactory` for creating different sensor widget types
- **Observer Pattern**: Implemented through Qt's signal-slot mechanism for real-time updates
- **Singleton Pattern**: Applied in `DatabaseManager` for centralized database access
- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation between UI components (Views), business logic, and data persistence

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[Business Logic Layer]
    B --> C[Data Access Layer]
    C --> D[(Database)]
    C --> E[MQTT Broker]
    E --> F[Raspberry Pi Sensors]
    
    G[Network Scanner] --> H[ARP Detection]
    H --> I[Device Discovery]
    I --> J[Status Updates]
    J --> B
```

### Data Flow Architecture

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant DB as Database Manager
    participant RM as Raspberry Manager
    participant MQTT as MQTT Broker
    participant RPI as Raspberry Pi
    
    U->>+DB: Authenticate User
    DB-->>-U: User Credentials Verified
    
    U->>+RM: Load Sensor Configuration
    RM->>+MQTT: Connect to Broker
    MQTT->>+RPI: Subscribe to Topics
    RPI-->>-MQTT: Sensor Data
    MQTT-->>-RM: Publish Data
    RM-->>-U: Real-time Updates
    
    U->>+DB: Log Actions
    DB-->>-U: Audit Trail
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Entry**: `main.cpp` - Initializes Qt application and displays main dashboard window
- **Dashboard Interface**: `dashboardwindow.h` - Central control panel managing all system components
- **User Authentication**: `loginwidget.h` - Secure login interface with role-based access control

### Core Modules

#### Database Management (`databasemanager.h`)
Centralized data persistence layer supporting:
- User account management with hashed passwords
- Role-based permission system
- Audit logging for security compliance
- MySQL/MariaDB compatibility

#### Sensor Management (`sensorfactory.h`)
Factory pattern implementation for creating specialized sensor widgets:
- Smoke sensors with gas concentration monitoring
- Temperature sensors with configurable thresholds
- Camera widgets with streaming capabilities
- Modular sensor configuration system

#### Network Management (`raspberrymanager.h`)
Distributed system coordination:
- MQTT broker configuration and connection management
- Raspberry Pi node discovery and status tracking
- Sensor configuration synchronization
- Automatic reconnection handling

#### Network Discovery (`arpscanner.h`)
Advanced network scanning capabilities:
- ARP table analysis for device detection
- MAC address vendor identification
- Known Raspberry Pi device recognition
- Real-time network topology mapping

### Configuration Approach
- **JSON Configuration**: `config/raspberry_nodes.json` - Centralized sensor and node definitions
- **Database Schema**: `database/surveillance_schema.sql` - Complete relational database structure
- **Build Configuration**: `applicationmalette.pro` - Qt project build settings with MySQL support

### External Dependencies
- **Qt Framework**: Core GUI and networking components
- **MySQL/MariaDB**: Primary database backend
- **MQTT Protocol**: Real-time sensor data communication
- **Qt SQL Module**: Database connectivity and ORM capabilities

### Integration Points
- **Hardware Integration**: Direct GPIO pin control for sensor interfaces
- **Network Integration**: MQTT broker communication for distributed sensor networks
- **Database Integration**: Persistent storage for user accounts, configurations, and audit logs
- **Security Integration**: Role-based access control with password hashing

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[DashboardWindow] --> B[DatabaseManager]
    A --> C[RaspberryManager]
    A --> D[ArpScanner]
    
    B --> E[(MySQL Database)]
    C --> F[MQTT Broker]
    C --> G[Raspberry Pi Nodes]
    
    H[SensorFactory] --> I[SmokeSensorWidget]
    H --> J[TemperatureWidget]
    H --> K[CameraWidget]
    
    L[NetworkScannerDialog] --> M[ArpScanner]
    L --> N[Detected Devices]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Multi-Level Security System
- **Admin Role**: Full system access with user management capabilities
- **Operator Role**: Sensor configuration and monitoring permissions
- **Viewer Role**: Read-only access to monitoring dashboards

#### Real-time Sensor Monitoring
- **Environmental Sensors**: Temperature, humidity, CO2, VOC, and smoke detection
- **Visual Surveillance**: Live camera streams with snapshot capabilities
- **Threshold-based Alerts**: Configurable warning and alarm notifications
- **Historical Data Analysis**: Time-series data storage and trend analysis

#### Distributed Network Management
- **Automated Device Discovery**: Network scanning with device identification
- **Dynamic Node Management**: Add/remove Raspberry Pi nodes at runtime
- **Load Balancing**: Intelligent distribution of monitoring tasks
- **Failover Mechanisms**: Redundant connections and automatic recovery

### Implementation Highlights

#### Widget System Architecture (`widgeteditor.h`)
- **Drag-and-Drop Interface**: Intuitive sensor placement and arrangement
- **Resizable Components**: Flexible widget sizing for optimal screen utilization
- **Edit Mode**: Contextual editing tools for sensor configuration
- **Lock Overlay**: Security features preventing unauthorized modifications

#### Network Discovery System (`networkscannerdialog.h`)
- **Interactive Scanning**: Real-time progress visualization
- **Device Classification**: Automatic categorization of discovered devices
- **Connection Management**: Secure connection establishment protocols
- **Status Monitoring**: Continuous device availability tracking

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Valid Credentials
    Authenticated --> Admin: Admin Role
    Authenticated --> Operator: Operator Role
    Authenticated --> Viewer: Viewer Role
    
    Admin --> Admin: Continue Admin
    Operator --> Operator: Continue Operator
    Viewer --> Viewer: Continue Viewer
    
    Authenticated --> Unauthenticated: Logout
    Unauthenticated --> [*]: Exit Application
    
    Admin --> EditMode: Enter Edit Mode
    Operator --> ViewMode: Enter View Mode
    Viewer --> ViewMode: Enter View Mode
    
    EditMode --> ViewMode: Exit Edit Mode
    ViewMode --> EditMode: Enter Edit Mode
```

#### Data Visualization and Alerting
- **Real-time Charts**: Interactive graphs showing sensor data trends
- **Color-coded Status**: Visual indicators for normal/warning/alarm states
- **Alert Notifications**: Multi-channel alert distribution (email, SMS, dashboard)
- **Historical Reports**: Comprehensive audit trails and compliance documentation

#### System Configuration Management
- **Dynamic Reconfiguration**: Runtime modification of system parameters
- **Backup and Restore**: Complete system state preservation
- **Version Control**: Change tracking and rollback capabilities
- **Remote Management**: Centralized administration across distributed nodes

---

**Sources:**
- [main.cpp](main.cpp)
- [dashboardwindow.h](dashboardwindow.h)
- [databasemanager.h](databasemanager.h)
- [sensorfactory.h](sensorfactory.h)
- [raspberrymanager.h](raspberrymanager.h)
- [config/raspberry_nodes.json](config/raspberry_nodes.json)
- [modulemanager.h](modulemanager.h)
- [applicationmalette.pro](applicationmalette.pro)
- [database/surveillance_schema.sql](database/surveillance_schema.sql)
- [smokesensorwidget.h](smokesensorwidget.h)
- [camerawidget.h](camerawidget.h)
- [loginwidget.h](loginwidget.h)
- [arpscanner.h](arpscanner.h)
- [networkscannerdialog.h](networkscannerdialog.h)

</blog>
